The NWS survey team determined that the damage found was consistent with straight line winds, with maximum wind gusts estimated to be between 80 and 90 mph. The most significant damage occurred to a barn which was destroyed, just off of Atkins Lane, about 7 miles southwest of Corbin, Kentucky. There were numerous trees uprooted between Frankfort School Road and Bee Creek Road, and from there to Linda Lane, just off of Casey Road. Several homes sustained damage from trees that fell on the roofs. Of notable interest were eyewitness reports of a circulation that tracked across a small pond in one of the residential areas. Based on the description of the circulation and location of this event, over a pond approximately 300 yards off the flanking (west) side of the core of most destructive winds, the NWS believes this may have been a book end vortex.
